subject: Physics
title: The Photoelectric Effect
description: A fast breakdown of The Photoelectric Effect for builders.

hook.text: Shine ultraviolet light on metal and electrons fly off. Shine a brighter red light and nothing happens.

buildup.text: Classical wave theory said brighter light should always knock out electrons. But it didn't work that way.

discovery.text: Einstein said each photon carries energy proportional to its frequency. Red photons are too weak regardless of number.

twist.text: This was quantum physics' first real-world proof. It earned Einstein his Nobel Prize in 1921.

climax.text: The photoelectric effect powers solar panels—photons knock electrons into a circuit, creating electricity.

punchline.text: Color matters more than brightness. Photons carry energy by frequency.

quiz.question: Why can't bright red light eject electrons from metal?
options: Each red photon lacks enough energy regardless of brightness
Red light is reflected by all metals
Electrons only respond to invisible light
